title: spec_driven_query_formulation_v1
description: spec_driven_v1 N2 Query Formulation output. queries = one search query per slot. query_text = BM25 lexical anchors (literal keywords + explicit_reference tokens verbatim). Each query carries scope channels, every one with a value + a {boost|filter} mode: collection (corpus document family), status (regulatory currency — RG/SRP/DSRS only), design (NuScale design family — nuscale_* only), canonical_id (a normalized document id for exact version targeting). status applies ONLY to regulatory collections and design ONLY to NuScale collections — the two are mutually exclusive (the code drops a channel set on the wrong collection).

reasoning.description: 1-2 sentence rationale written *before* building the queries (the query's language is fine): which slots / explicit references you map to which lexical anchors / collection. Guided decoding decodes this field first, so the subsequent queries are conditioned on it (leading CoT).

collection_mode.description: How to apply collection: 'boost' = additive in-scope boost, excludes nothing (recall-safe, default); 'filter' = hard-restrict the search population to that collection (narrows recall — only when confident the answer lives only there).

status.description: Regulatory currency of the document. Applies ONLY to RG/SRP/DSRS collections (10CFR/FR and NuScale documents have no status — leave null there). Default to 'current' unless the query explicitly asks for revision history / a past edition / a draft / a withdrawn version.

status_mode.description: How to apply status: 'boost' (default, additive) or 'filter' (hard-restrict to that status — use when confident, e.g. 'the current requirement').

design.description: NuScale design family. Applies ONLY to nuscale_* collections (regulatory documents have no design — leave null there). US600 = the certified Design Certification Application (DCA, ~50 MWe/module); US460 = the later Standard Design Approval Application (SDAA, uprated ~77 MWe/module); PreApp = pre-application-stage documents that predate the DCA. Default to 'US600' (the certified baseline design) unless the query explicitly names US460 / the SDAA / Docket 05200050, or pre-application material.

design_mode.description: How to apply design: 'boost' (default, additive — does not exclude the other design) or 'filter' (hard-restrict to one design — use when the query is clearly about a single design family).

canonical_id.description: A normalized document id for version/section targeting. NRC_MANUAL forms: RG-{division}.{num} (RG-1.206), SRP-{ch}.{sec} (SRP-15.6.5), DSRS-{sec} (DSRS-10.3), 10CFR-Part{N} for a single 10 CFR Part (10 CFR 50.46 -> 10CFR-Part50, 10 CFR Part 52 -> 10CFR-Part52, GDC -> 10CFR-Part50, 10 CFR 100.11 -> 10CFR-Part100; do NOT emit the bundled-volume form 10CFR-Part1-50 — the code maps the Part to its volume and narrows to that Part's page span). NuScale FSAR forms (nuscale_FSAR slot only): FSAR-Part02-Ch{N} to scope to a Tier-2 chapter 1-21 (map the topic to the chapter, e.g. ECCS->Ch06, accident analysis->Ch15), or FSAR-Part{01,07,08,09,10} for a non-technical part. Revision is NOT included. Do NOT invent one for title-keyword documents (Letter/Meeting/Email). null when not confidently mappable. The code re-validates the form, the chapter range, the 10 CFR Part->page map, and the collection match, dropping it on failure (so a wrong guess is harmless).

canonical_id_mode.description: How to apply canonical_id: 'boost' (default, additive) or 'filter' (hard-restrict to that exact document grouping — use when the query unambiguously names that one document).
